name: Phil Taylor
highlights: 16x World Champion — an all-time record
11 perfect legs in televised play — more than anyone else
72-match unbeaten run in 2001
First player to average over 100 in a televised match
6 consecutive World Championship titles (1995-2002)
quote: I just love darts. I eat, sleep and breathe it.
fun_fact: Taylor was handed his first set of darts by Eric Bristow, who also personally sponsored his early career — without that moment, darts history looks very different.
bio: Widely regarded as the greatest darts player of all time, Phil Taylor dominated the sport for over two decades. Born in Stoke-on-Trent, he claimed a record 16 World Championship titles and is credited with transforming darts into the mainstream sport it is today.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Phil Taylor — 16x World Champion and the greatest darts player who ever lived. #DartsDaily

name: Michael van Gerwen
walk_on_music: Seven Nation Army — The White Stripes
highlights: 3x PDC World Champion (2014, 2017, 2019)
Highest ever televised average: 123.40
9 televised nine-dart finishes
Record 7 Premier League Darts titles
Youngest ever PDC ranking event winner at age 16
quote: I'm not arrogant. I'm confident. There's a difference.
fun_fact: Van Gerwen won his first PDC ranking event aged just 16 — making him the youngest winner in PDC history. He was still in school at the time.
bio: The most dominant player of the modern era, Michael van Gerwen has redefined what is possible in darts. His scoring power and finishing ability are unmatched, and he continues to set records that were once thought impossible.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Michael van Gerwen — 3x World Champion and the most feared player on tour. #DartsDaily

name: Luke Littler
highlights: PDC World Champion 2025 — aged just 17
Reached the 2024 World Championship final aged 16
Became the youngest player to win a PDC ranking event
Won the 2024 World Series of Darts Finals
Averaged 130+ in multiple televised matches
quote: I just play my game. I don't really think about the pressure.
fun_fact: Littler's favourite food is kebab — a fact that became a running joke and viral moment during his 2024 World Championship run, spawning countless memes.
bio: The teenage sensation who took the darts world by storm. Luke Littler burst onto the scene at the 2024 PDC World Championship as a 16-year-old, reaching the final and capturing the hearts of millions before going on to win the title a year later.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Luke Littler — The Nuke. World Champion at 17 and the future of darts. #DartsDaily

name: Fallon Sherrock
highlights: First woman ever to beat a man at the PDC World Championship (2019)
Beat two men at the 2019 Worlds — back to back
Multiple Women's Series event winner
Sparked a global surge in women taking up darts
Featured in Time magazine's 100 most influential people
quote: I just wanted to show that women belong on that stage.
fun_fact: Fallon became a mother at 16 and used darts as a creative outlet. She went on to make history on the sport's biggest stage just a few years later.
bio: Fallon Sherrock rewrote the history books at the 2019 PDC World Championship, becoming the first woman ever to beat a man at the tournament. Her courage inspired millions and opened doors for a new generation of players.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Fallon Sherrock — The Queen of the Palace and the woman who made darts history. #DartsDaily

name: Gary Anderson
highlights: 2x PDC World Champion (2015, 2016)
Back-to-back World titles in consecutive years
Multiple Premier League Darts titles
Known for one of the most unorthodox throwing styles in the game
Overcame serious back and shoulder injuries throughout career
quote: I never think I'm going to lose. I always believe I can win.
fun_fact: Anderson's throwing style breaks every coaching rule — his elbow drops as he throws. Yet that unorthodox technique produced two World Championship titles.
bio: Gary Anderson is one of the most naturally talented players ever to pick up a dart. His relaxed demeanour and unconventional technique belie a fierce competitor who has claimed two World Championship titles.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Gary Anderson — The Flying Scotsman. 2x World Champion with one of the most unique throwing styles in darts. #DartsDaily

name: Raymond van Barneveld
highlights: 5x World Champion (4x BDO, 1x PDC)
Defeated Phil Taylor 7-6 in the greatest World Championship final ever (2007)
First Dutch player to win a World Championship
Multiple Grand Prix and UK Open titles
Came out of retirement in 2022 to rejoin the PDC tour
quote: When I beat Taylor that night, I felt like I was walking on air.
fun_fact: Before darts, Barneveld worked as a postman in The Hague. He practised his darts in lunch breaks and after his daily deliveries.
bio: One of the most beloved figures in darts history, Raymond van Barneveld won five World Championships across two tours. His legendary 7-6 victory over Phil Taylor in 2007 is widely considered the greatest match ever played.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Raymond van Barneveld — 5x World Champion and hero of the greatest darts final ever. #DartsDaily

name: Peter Wright
highlights: 2x PDC World Champion (2020, 2022)
Known for the most colourful hair and outfits in darts history
Multiple ranking event winner across his career
Did not win his first major until his 40s — the ultimate late bloomer
Grand Prix and Masters champion
quote: The hair, the outfit — it's all part of who I am. I want people to enjoy the show.
fun_fact: Wright's wife Joanne designs many of his outfits and hairstyles. He once arrived at a tournament with his hair styled as the Scottish flag.
bio: Peter Wright is one of the most colourful characters in darts — literally. The two-time World Champion is as famous for his wild hair and outfits as he is for his dart throwing, but behind the showmanship is one of the most complete players on tour.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Peter Wright — Snakebite! 2x World Champion and the most colourful character in darts. #DartsDaily

name: Eric Bristow
highlights: Dominated darts throughout the 1980s
Personally launched the career of Phil Taylor
One of the founding fathers of modern professional darts
Awarded an MBE for services to darts in 1989
quote: I was the best. Simple as that.
fun_fact: Bristow personally sponsored a young Phil Taylor's entry into professional darts — effectively creating his own greatest ever rival and successor.
bio: Eric Bristow was the defining figure of darts in the 1980s. The Crafty Cockney won five World Championship titles and was the sport's biggest personality for over a decade, helping bring darts into the mainstream on British television.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Eric Bristow — The Crafty Cockney. 5x World Champion and one of darts all-time greats. #DartsDaily

name: John Lowe
highlights: 3x World Champion (1979, 1987, 1993)
Hit the first ever televised nine-dart finish (1984)
Won 102,000 pounds for that perfect leg — a record prize at the time
Competed professionally across five decades
One of the true founding fathers of televised darts
quote: That nine-darter? I knew what I was doing every single dart.
fun_fact: When John Lowe hit the first televised nine-dart finish in 1984, he won 102,000 pounds in prize money. He reportedly stayed completely calm throughout the entire leg.
bio: John Lowe is a true legend of darts who competed at the highest level for nearly three decades. Best known for throwing the first ever televised nine-dart finish in 1984, Old Stoneface won three World Championship titles and helped shape the modern game.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: John Lowe — Old Stoneface. 3x World Champion and the man who threw the first ever televised nine-dart finish. #DartsDaily

name: Adrian Lewis
highlights: 2x PDC World Champion (2011, 2012)
Won back-to-back World titles aged 25 and 26
Close friend and protege of Phil Taylor
Multiple ranking event winner on the PDC tour
One of Stoke-on-Trent's most celebrated sporting exports
quote: When you walk out at Alexandra Palace, nothing else matters.
fun_fact: Lewis is a lifelong close friend of Phil Taylor — both from Stoke-on-Trent. Taylor was a major influence on Lewis from a very young age.
bio: Adrian Lewis burst onto the scene with back-to-back PDC World Championship titles in 2011 and 2012. Nicknamed Jackpot for his explosive winning ability, he remains one of the most talented players of his generation.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Adrian Lewis — Jackpot! 2x World Champion and one of darts most explosive finishers. #DartsDaily

name: Gerwyn Price
highlights: PDC World Champion 2021
Former professional rugby player before switching to darts
Premier League Darts champion
Grand Slam of Darts champion
Rose from Q-School to World Champion in just 7 years
quote: I don't care what people think. I'm here to win.
fun_fact: Before darts, Price played professional rugby union in Wales. He only switched to darts full time in his late 20s, making his World title even more remarkable.
bio: Gerwyn Price is one of the most intense and driven competitors in darts. The Iceman swapped rugby boots for tungsten and went on to become PDC World Champion in 2021, proving it is never too late to reach the top.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Gerwyn Price — The Iceman. PDC World Champion and former professional rugby player. #DartsDaily

name: Jonny Clayton
highlights: Premier League Darts champion 2021
Grand Slam of Darts champion 2021
World Cup winner with Wales alongside Gerwyn Price
Worked as a plasterer before turning professional
Known for one of the quickest throwing actions on tour
quote: I never dreamed I would be here. I just kept working.
fun_fact: Clayton worked as a plasterer and part-time darts player for years before turning professional. He won the Premier League and Grand Slam in the same year he finally went full time.
bio: Jonny Clayton is the ultimate underdog story in darts. The Ferret from Pembrey worked in construction while playing darts semi-professionally before breaking through to win multiple major titles in 2021.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Jonny Clayton — The Ferret. From plasterer to Premier League champion. #DartsDaily

name: Jose de Sousa
walk_on_music: Pitbull - I Know You Want Me
highlights: PDC World Champion 2021
Grand Slam of Darts champion 2020
First Portuguese player to win a PDC World title
Known for calculating finishes mentally without a chalk board
Turned professional at age 34 and reached the world final within 3 years
quote: I calculate everything in my head. The numbers just come to me.
fun_fact: De Sousa is famous for calculating his finishes entirely in his head without needing to look at a scoreboard — a rare gift that has baffled opponents and commentators alike.
bio: Jose de Sousa is one of the most remarkable stories in modern darts. Turning professional at 34, The Special One became PDC World Champion within three years — a stunning achievement that captured the imagination of fans worldwide.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Jose de Sousa — The Special One. PDC World Champion and Portugal's greatest ever darts player. #DartsDaily

name: Dimitri Van den Bergh
walk_on_music: David Guetta feat. Ne-Yo & Akon - Play Hard
highlights: PDC World Youth Champion 2018
Grand Slam of Darts champion 2020
Belgium's most successful darts player of all time
Known for his trademark celebration after big finishes
Multiple European Tour event winner
quote: I play with my heart. Every single dart.
fun_fact: Van den Bergh is known for his wild celebrations after hitting big checkouts — he once did a full theatrical performance on stage after winning a leg, much to the crowd's delight.
bio: Dimitri Van den Bergh is Belgian darts personified — passionate, creative and full of flair. The DreamMaker has put Belgium firmly on the darts map and is one of the most entertaining players to watch on the PDC tour.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Dimitri Van den Bergh — The DreamMaker. Belgium's greatest darts player and one of the most entertaining on tour. #DartsDaily

name: Dave Chisnall
walk_on_music: Vic Reeves & The Wonder Stuff - Dizzy
highlights: Multiple PDC ranking event winner
Consistent top-16 player for over a decade
Known for some of the highest scoring legs on the PDC tour
Defeated Phil Taylor at Alexandra Palace — one of the great upsets
Beloved by fans for his friendly demeanour and consistent entertainment
quote: I just love playing darts in front of a big crowd. It's the best feeling.
fun_fact: Chisnall famously beat Phil Taylor at the PDC World Championship — one of the biggest upsets in the tournament's history at the time. The Ally Pally crowd went absolutely wild.
bio: Dave Chisnall has been one of the most consistent performers on the PDC tour for over a decade. Chizzy is a crowd favourite known for his flowing throwing action, high scoring and warm personality.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Dave Chisnall — Chizzy! One of the PDC tour's most consistent and entertaining performers. #DartsDaily

name: Simon Whitlock
highlights: PDC World Championship finalist 2011
Multiple World Cup wins representing Australia
One of the most recognisable figures in darts with his trademark beard and hat
Consistent top-32 player for over 15 years
Ambassador for darts growth across Asia and the Pacific
quote: The crowd gives me energy. When they're behind me, I feel unstoppable.
fun_fact: Whitlock's giant beard and cowboy hat make him one of the most instantly recognisable players in world darts — fans often dress up as him at tournaments as a tribute.
bio: Simon Whitlock has been Australia's darts ambassador to the world for over 15 years. The Wizard reached the PDC World Championship final in 2011 and has remained a consistent and beloved presence on the world stage ever since.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Simon Whitlock — The Wizard. Australia's greatest darts export and a true fan favourite. #DartsDaily

name: Mensur Suljovic
highlights: European Championship finalist
Multiple European Tour event winner
One of the slowest and most deliberate throwers on tour
Serbia's most successful darts player of all time
Consistently ranked inside the world's top 32 for a decade
quote: I take my time. Every dart deserves full attention.
fun_fact: Suljovic is one of the slowest throwers in professional darts — his deliberate, almost painfully slow routine has frustrated opponents and delighted neutrals in equal measure.
bio: Mensur Suljovic has carried the flag for Serbian darts for over a decade. The Gentle is known for his extraordinarily deliberate throwing style and has been one of Europe's most consistent performers on the PDC circuit.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Mensur Suljovic — The Gentle. Serbia's darts pioneer and one of the tour's most unique characters. #DartsDaily

name: Glen Durrant
highlights: 3x BDO World Champion (2017, 2018, 2019)
Premier League Darts champion 2020
Won three consecutive BDO World titles — a remarkable achievement
Switched to the PDC after dominating the BDO
Known for his calm, unflappable temperament under pressure
quote: Three world titles. Nobody can ever take that away from me.
fun_fact: Durrant won three BDO World Championship titles in a row — a stunning run of dominance that earned him comparisons to the legends of the sport before he had even joined the PDC.
bio: Glen Durrant is a three-time BDO World Champion who dominated that circuit before making the switch to the PDC, where he won the Premier League title in 2020. Duzza is proof that brilliance can emerge at any stage of a career.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Glen Durrant — Duzza! 3x BDO World Champion and Premier League title winner. #DartsDaily

name: Andy Hamilton
walk_on_music: MC Hammer - U Can't Touch This
highlights: PDC World Championship finalist 2012
Defeated multiple top-10 players on route to the final
Known for his powerful, no-nonsense approach to the game
Fan favourite for his down-to-earth personality
Represented England in multiple World Cup tournaments
quote: I just throw my darts and see what happens. No fuss.
fun_fact: Hamilton's walk-on music — Hammer Time by MC Hammer — became one of the most beloved walk-on songs in darts, with Ally Pally crowds singing along every time he stepped up.
bio: Andy Hamilton is one of darts' most loveable characters. The Hammer reached the PDC World Championship final in 2012 and has been a popular presence on the tour for many years, known for his honest, no-nonsense style both on and off the oche.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Andy Hamilton — The Hammer! PDC World finalist and one of darts most popular personalities. #DartsDaily

name: Terry Jenkins
highlights: PDC World Championship semi-finalist on multiple occasions
Consistent top-32 presence for over a decade
Known for his reliable, methodical approach
One of the most experienced players of his era
Represented England with distinction throughout his career
quote: Consistency wins matches. Flashy darts don't always win.
fun_fact: Jenkins was one of the most reliable and consistent performers of his era — never a world champion, but always competitive and respected by every player he faced.
bio: Terry Jenkins was a dependable and experienced presence on the PDC circuit for many years. The Bull never quite captured a world title but reached the semi-finals on multiple occasions and was respected throughout the sport.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Terry Jenkins — The Bull. One of darts most consistent and respected performers. #DartsDaily

name: Kevin Painter
walk_on_music: The Greatest Showman - The Greatest Show
highlights: PDC World Championship finalist 2004
Took Phil Taylor to a deciding set in that final — a stunning performance
One of the most aggressive throwers of his generation
UK Open quarter-finalist multiple times
Known for his electric walk-on and crowd connection
quote: I nearly had him. I was right there.
fun_fact: Painter came within a whisker of defeating Phil Taylor in the 2004 PDC World Championship final — one of the closest finals in the tournament's history. Taylor required a deciding set to see him off.
bio: Kevin Painter is best remembered for pushing Phil Taylor all the way in the 2004 PDC World Championship final. The Artist was one of the most exciting and aggressive players of his era and a true fan favourite.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Kevin Painter — The Artist. The man who nearly beat Phil Taylor in a World Championship final. #DartsDaily

name: Mark Webster
highlights: BDO World Champion 2008
Represented Wales in multiple World Cup tournaments
Switched from BDO to PDC and maintained competitive level
Known for his consistent and reliable finishing
One of Wales' most decorated darts players alongside Price and Clayton
quote: Wales is producing world-class darts players and I am proud to be part of that.
fun_fact: Webster won the BDO World Championship in 2008 — at the time one of the biggest upsets in that tournament's history. He has since represented Wales with distinction across both tours.
bio: Mark Webster is a BDO World Champion and proud Welsh representative who has competed at the highest level of the sport for two decades. The Spider is a consistent, reliable competitor and a key figure in Welsh darts history.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Mark Webster — The Spider. BDO World Champion and Welsh darts legend. #DartsDaily

name: Andy Fordham
walk_on_music: Right Said Fred — I'm Too Sexy
highlights: BDO World Champion 2004
One of the most recognisable figures in 2000s darts
Known for his imposing physical presence and large darts
Overcame serious health issues to continue competing
Beloved by fans for his warmth and good humour
quote: I loved every minute of it. Every crowd, every match.
fun_fact: Fordham used some of the heaviest darts in professional play at 28g — significantly heavier than most professionals. His powerful throwing style was perfectly matched to the weight.
bio: Andy Fordham was one of the most beloved figures in British darts during the 2000s. The Viking won the BDO World Championship in 2004 and was known as much for his larger-than-life personality as for his darts ability.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Andy Fordham — The Viking! BDO World Champion and one of darts most beloved characters. #DartsDaily

name: Dennis Priestley
highlights: 2x World Champion (BDO 1991, PDC 1994)
One of only a handful of players to win both BDO and PDC World titles
Founding member of the WDC breakaway in 1993
Played alongside the greatest players of two eras
A respected figure in darts history for his role in shaping the modern game
quote: We took a risk leaving the BDO. But it was the right decision.
fun_fact: Priestley was one of the original rebels who left the BDO in 1993 to form what became the PDC. Without that breakaway, modern professional darts as we know it might never have existed.
bio: Dennis Priestley is a two-time World Champion and a key figure in darts history. As one of the founding members of the PDC breakaway, he helped shape the professional game that millions now enjoy worldwide.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Dennis Priestley — The Menace. 2x World Champion and one of the founding fathers of the PDC. #DartsDaily

name: Rod Harrington
highlights: 2x World Matchplay champion (1998, 1999)
Multiple PDC ranking event winner throughout the 1990s
Known for one of the most stylish throwing actions in the game
Top-8 player throughout his peak years
Respected pundit and analyst after retiring from playing
quote: Darts should look good as well as feel good. Style matters.
fun_fact: Harrington's throwing action was so smooth and stylish that players would actively watch him warm up just to study his technique. Coaches still use footage of him to this day.
bio: Rod Harrington was one of the smoothest and most stylish players of his era. The Prince of Style won multiple major titles in the 1990s and is remembered as much for the elegance of his game as for his results.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Rod Harrington — The Prince of Style. One of the smoothest throwers and most stylish players in darts history. #DartsDaily

name: Wayne Mardle
highlights: World Grand Prix finalist
Multiple PDC ranking event winner
One of the most entertaining walk-ons in darts history
Beloved TV pundit and commentator after retiring
Known for his flamboyant shirts and extrovert personality
quote: Darts is entertainment. Give the crowd what they want.
fun_fact: Mardle's Hawaii Five-O walk-on became one of the most iconic in darts — he would strut to the oche in a Hawaiian shirt while the crowd went wild. He turned a TV theme into a darts anthem.
bio: Wayne Mardle was one of the most entertaining figures in darts during his playing career. Hawaii 501 brought colour, personality and passion to every tournament he entered and has since become one of the sport's most respected analysts.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Wayne Mardle — Hawaii 501! One of darts most entertaining characters both on and off the oche. #DartsDaily

name: John Part
highlights: 3x World Champion (BDO 1994, PDC 2003, PDC 2008)
First North American player to win a World Darts Championship
Won titles across three different decades
One of the most technically precise throwers in history
A global ambassador for darts in North America
quote: Canada on the world darts stage. Nobody expected it. That made it even better.
fun_fact: Part was the first North American player to win a World Darts Championship — an astonishing achievement that put Canada on the global darts map and inspired a generation of players across the continent.
bio: John Part is Canada's greatest ever darts player and one of the sport's true legends. Darth Maple won three World Championship titles across three different decades — a remarkable feat that places him among the all-time greats.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: John Part — Darth Maple! Canada's 3x World Darts Champion and North America's greatest ever player. #DartsDaily

name: Tony O'Shea
highlights: BDO World Championship finalist 2009 and 2013
One of the oldest players to reach a World Championship final
Represented England with distinction over many years
Known for his incredible consistency deep into his career
A fan favourite for his friendly manner and fighting spirit
quote: Age is just a number. If you can throw, you can compete.
fun_fact: O'Shea reached the BDO World Championship final in 2013 at the age of 53 — making him one of the oldest finalists in the history of any World Darts Championship.
bio: Tony O'Shea is proof that darts has no age limit. The Tornado reached two BDO World Championship finals, including one at the age of 53, and was a beloved and respected figure throughout his long career.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Tony O'Shea — The Tornado! Two-time BDO World finalist who proved age is no barrier in darts. #DartsDaily

name: Martin Adams
walk_on_music: Duran Duran - Hungry Like The Wolf
highlights: 3x BDO World Champion (2007, 2010, 2011)
Won his first World title at age 50 — one of the oldest champions ever
Captain of the England World Cup team for many years
A respected ambassador and administrator for the BDO
Known for his iconic walk-on and warm personality
quote: I won my first world title at 50. It is never too late.
fun_fact: Adams won his first BDO World Championship at the age of 50 — making him one of the oldest first-time world champions in darts history. A stunning achievement and an inspiration to players everywhere.
bio: Martin Adams is a three-time BDO World Champion and one of the most respected figures in the history of the sport. Wolfie won his first world title at 50 and his story remains one of the most inspiring in darts.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Martin Adams — Wolfie! 3x BDO World Champion who won his first title at age 50. #DartsDaily

name: Scott Waites
highlights: 2x BDO World Champion (2007, 2009)
Multiple BDO ranking event winner
Known for his consistency and mental strength
One of Bradford's most celebrated sporting exports
Competed at the highest level of the BDO for over a decade
quote: Two world titles. Bradford boy done good.
fun_fact: Waites is a two-time BDO World Champion from Bradford who became a local hero in his hometown. His cheerful nickname Scotty 2 Hotty perfectly captured the personality he brought to every tournament.
bio: Scott Waites is a two-time BDO World Champion and one of the most consistent performers of the BDO era. Scotty 2 Hotty brought energy, personality and excellence to every stage he walked onto.
share_text: Darts Daily Player of the Day: Scott Waites — Scotty 2 Hotty! 2x BDO World Champion from Bradford. #DartsDaily

name: Luke Humphries
walk_on_music: Kaiser Chiefs - I Predict a Riot
highlights: PDC World Champion 2024
Won three major televised titles in 2023 (Grand Slam, Players Championship Finals, World Grand Prix)
Became PDC world number one in 2024
Runner-up at 2022 UK Open
Four European Tour titles (2022–2023)
quote: Belief is everything. If you work hard, anything is possible.
fun_fact: Humphries won his first PDC World Championship in 2024 and is known for his rapid rise from Open tour winner to world number one.
bio: Luke Humphries is England’s top-ranked dart player and 2024 PDC World Champion. Known as “Cool Hand Luke,” he rose rapidly from Development Tour winner to world number one, claiming multiple major titles and earning a reputation for calm under pressure.
share_text: DartsDaily.net Player of the Day: Luke Humphries — Cool Hand. 2024 World Champion and the rising star of English darts. #DartsDaily

name: Gian van Veen
highlights: Won PDC Tour Card at 2023 Q-School
Reached first PDC televised quarter-final (2023 Players Championship Finals)
Youngest Dutch player in the PDC Top 32 (2024)
Defeated multiple seeded players in European Tour events
Progressed rapidly from Development Tour (2022) to the main circuit
quote: I just want to show the world what I can do.
fun_fact: Van Veen reached his first PDC televised quarter-final within a year of earning his Tour Card, marking him as one of the fastest-rising Dutch stars.
bio: Gian van Veen is a young Dutch star who earned his PDC Tour Card in 2023. With quick success on the European circuit and a televised quarter-final, he’s regarded as one of the fastest-rising talents in darts.
share_text: DartsDaily.net Player of the Day: Gian van Veen — The Dutch sensation lighting up the PDC tour with his fearless approach. #DartsDaily

name: James Wade
highlights: Winner of 11 major PDC titles including World Matchplay, UK Open, and World Grand Prix
First left-handed player to win a PDC major
Ranked in PDC Top 10 for over 15 consecutive years
Runner-up in PDC World Championship three times
One of only three players to beat Phil Taylor in a World Matchplay final
quote: It's all about consistency—and I back myself every single time.
fun_fact: Wade is the only player other than Phil Taylor to win the World Matchplay, World Grand Prix, and UK Open, all without winning the PDC World Championship.
bio: James Wade has been a mainstay of world darts for over 15 years. “The Machine” is noted for his clinical finishing and consistency, having won multiple majors—including the World Matchplay, World Grand Prix, and UK Open.
share_text: DartsDaily.net Player of the Day: James Wade — The Machine. Multiple major winner and one of darts’ most clinical finishers. #DartsDaily

name: Stephen Bunting
highlights: BDO World Champion 2014
Multiple PDC major semi-finals, including World Championship (2021)
Winner of three PDC ranking titles
Consistently ranked in PDC Top 32 since 2014
Noted for quick, high-scoring playing style
quote: I play with a smile because I love the game.
fun_fact: Bunting is a former BDO World Champion and famously nicknamed “The Bullet” for his quick-fire throwing style.
bio: Stephen Bunting is a former BDO World Champion and a steady force on the PDC circuit. Nicknamed “The Bullet,” his quick throwing style and resilience have made him a perennial contender in major tournaments.
share_text: DartsDaily.net Player of the Day: Stephen Bunting — The Bullet. BDO World Champion and Premier League semi-finalist. #DartsDaily

name: Josh Rock
walk_on_music: Dj Krissy - Welcome to the Party
highlights: PDC World Youth Champion 2022
Hit televised nine-darter in World Youth final
Winner of first PDC senior ranking title in 2023
Quarter-finals in Players Championship Finals (2023)
Youngest player to hit a PDC nine-darter live on TV (2022)
quote: I'm here to enjoy it and make a statement for the next generation.
fun_fact: Rock became the youngest ever winner of the PDC World Youth Championship in 2022, hitting a nine-darter in the final.
bio: Josh Rock is one of the brightest young darts talents, capturing the 2022 PDC World Youth Championship and a nine-darter in the final. He’s already making waves on the senior tour.
share_text: DartsDaily.net Player of the Day: Josh Rock — Rockstar! World Youth Champion and one of darts’ most exciting prospects. #DartsDaily

name: Danny Noppert
walk_on_music: Panic! at the Disco - High Hopes
highlights: UK Open Champion 2022
BDO World finalist 2017
Twice reached PDC World Championship quarter-finals
Winner of multiple European Tour events
Top Dutch player after Van Gerwen and Barneveld
quote: Stay cool. Stay focused. That’s always my mindset.
fun_fact: Noppert won the UK Open in 2022, becoming only the second Dutchman to win a PDC major after van Barneveld and van Gerwen.
bio: Danny Noppert is the second Dutchman (after Van Gerwen and Barneveld) to win a PDC major, clinching the UK Open in 2022. Known as “The Freeze,” he’s one of the leading names in Dutch darts.
share_text: DartsDaily.net Player of the Day: Danny Noppert — The Freeze. UK Open Champion and consistently among the world’s elite. #DartsDaily

name: Ryan Searle
